channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: China's economy grows 4.3% in Q2, slowest since late 2022
item.description: Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: American AI is expensive. Some startups are turning to cheap Chinese models
item.description: AI is a fast-growing business expense. Some companies are cutting costs by switching to cheaper Chinese AI models.
item.content:encoded:
AI is a fast-growing business expense. Some companies are cutting costs by switching to cheaper Chinese AI models.

(Image credit: Imen Ben Youssef/Hans Lucas)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Mystery bidder buys T. rex nicknamed 'Gus' for a record $50 million
item.description: A T-rex fossil billed as one of the world's largest specimens was sold for a record $50.1 million Tuesday to a mystery bidder. But many aren't happy about it.
item.content:encoded:
A T-rex fossil billed as one of the world's largest specimens was sold for a record $50.1 million Tuesday to a mystery bidder. But many aren't happy about it.

(Image credit: Matthew Sherman/Sotheby's via AP)
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kim

item.title: Inflation slowed sharply -- but it may not last
item.description: Annual inflation hit 3.5% in June, down from May's more than three-year high — but the resumption of the conflict with Iran threaten to push up inflation as energy costs once again spike.
item.content:encoded:
Annual inflation hit 3.5% in June, down from May's more than three-year high — but the resumption of the conflict with Iran threaten to push up inflation as energy costs once again spike.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: 'The Trojan Teddy Bear': The promise and peril of childhood in the age of AI
item.description: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.
item.content:encoded: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: States sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros blockbuster merger
item.description: California is among the states suing to block Paramount from buying Warner Bros. Discovery in a Hollywood mega-merger that would unite some of the nation's largest movie studios and TV newsrooms.
item.content:encoded:
California is among the states suing to block Paramount from buying Warner Bros. Discovery in a Hollywood mega-merger that would unite some of the nation's largest movie studios and TV newsrooms.

(Image credit: Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: EV battery recycling has a math problem
item.description: For some electric vehicle batteries, it costs more to recycle them than they're worth. That creates all kinds of problems, which a new Colorado law aims to solve.
item.content:encoded: For some electric vehicle batteries, it costs more to recycle them than they're worth. That creates all kinds of problems, which a new Colorado law aims to solve.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: What a Monopoly importer learned when it tried to make things in the U.S.A.
item.description: After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.
item.content:encoded:
After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.

(Image credit: WS Game Company)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Justice Department subpoenas New York Times reporters over Air Force One reporting
item.description: The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.
item.content:encoded:
The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: 'Steal This Story, Please!' puts journalist Amy Goodman in the spotlight
item.description: The independently distributed documentary follows the Democracy Now! journalist's reporting career and the independent media model she built over three decades.
item.content:encoded:
The independently distributed documentary follows the Democracy Now! journalist's reporting career and the independent media model she built over three decades.

(Image credit: Mandalit del Barco)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco
